name: SK Sturm Graz at FC Midtjylland

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest drew 2-2 with Real Betis Manchester United in their first European match since the 1995-96 season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Antony rescues draw for Betis against Forest

headlines.description: Substitute Kelechi Iheanacho put Celtic ahead in Belgrade before they had to settle for a point against Red Star in their Europa League opener.
headlines.shortLinkText: Celtic frustrated by Red Star in Europa opener

headlines.description: John McGinn's first-half strike earned Aston Villa their first win of the season with a 1-0 victory over Bologna in their Europa League opener.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa beat Bologna for first win of season

headlines.description: Oh Hyeon-Gyu scored after missing an earlier penalty as Genk beat 10-man Rangers 1-0 in their Europa League opener.
headlines.shortLinkText: Pressure builds on Martin as Genk edge Rangers

headlines.description: Kelechi Iheanacho was on the wrong end of a hugely controversial handball decision before Braga wrapped up a 2-0 Europa League win over Celtic on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Celtic have goal ruled out in Sporting Braga loss

name: Red Star Belgrade at FC Porto

headlines.description: Emi Buendia and John McGinn struck second-half goals as Aston Villa beat Feyenoord 2-0 in Rotterdam to make it back-to-back Europa League wins.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa beat Feyenoord to stay perfect in Europa

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest suffered defeat in their first European game at the City Ground in almost 30 years as the home fans turned on new boss Ange Postecoglou during a 3-2 Europa League loss to Midtjylland.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ange feels Forest fans fury in Europa League loss

headlines.description: Rangers showed some fight but first-half defensive lapses saw them go down 2-1 to Sturm Graz in a second Europa League defeat in eight days on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rangers, Martin slump to latest loss at Graz

name: Aston Villa at Go Ahead Eagles

headlines.description: Aston Villa suffered a Europa League humbling as they were beaten 2-1 by Dutch minnows Go Ahead Eagles in Deventer.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa suffer Europa humbling by Go Ahead Eagles

headlines.description: Two goals in three second-half minutes got Celtic motoring in the Europa League as Brendan Rodgers' side came from behind to beat 10-man Sturm Graz 2-1.
headlines.shortLinkText: Celtic fight back to claim first Europa League win

headlines.description: Morgan Gibbs-White and Igor Jesus' penalties sealed Nottingham Forest a first European victory in 30 years as life under Sean Dyche began with a much-needed 2-0 win against FC Porto.
headlines.shortLinkText: Dyche starts Forest tenure with Europa League win

headlines.description: Celtic's revival under Martin O'Neill came juddering to a halt in Jutland after they were thumped 3-1 by FC Midtjylland.
headlines.shortLinkText: Celtic fall to sobering European defeat in Denmark

name: Go Ahead Eagles at RB Salzburg
Nottingham Forest at SK Sturm Graz

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest remain unbeaten away from home in the Europa League after they battled to a goalless draw with Sturm Graz at the Merkur Arena.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gibbs-White misses pen as Forest draw in Austria

headlines.description: Aston Villa coasted to a 2-0 Europa League win over Maccabi Tel Aviv where all the attention was on what happened off the pitch.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa ease past Maccabi in Europa League

headlines.description: Rangers' Europa League troubles continued with a 2-0 defeat by a superior Roma side at Ibrox.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rangers stay rock-bottom of UEL after Roma loss

headlines.description: Donyell Malen struck a first-half brace as Aston Villa beat Young Boys 2-1 in the Europa League amid trouble from the away support at Villa Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fan violence mars Aston Villa win over Young Boys

headlines.description: Martin O'Neill wrote a memorable final postscript in his history with Celtic in Europe as the interim manager led his team to a 3-1 win over Feyenoord.
headlines.shortLinkText: Celtic record big win at Van Persie's Feyenoord

name: VfB Stuttgart at Go Ahead Eagles

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest paid tribute to their 1979 European Cup-winning heroes in style with a 3-0 win over Malmö in the Europa League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest rout Malmö in European Cup final rematch

headlines.description: James Tavernier scored a penalty as Rangers earned their first Europa League point of the season with a 1-1 draw at home to 10-man Braga.
headlines.shortLinkText: Two red cards as Rangers held by Braga

headlines.description: Igor Jesus' late goal sealed Nottingham Forest a 2-1 win in Utrecht and boosted their hopes of a top-eight finish in the Europa League group phase.
headlines.shortLinkText: Igor Jesus' late goal helps Nottingham Forest beat Utrecht

headlines.description: Danny Rohl's Rangers again found the Europa League too much of a burden with a 2-1 defeat by Ferencvaros in Budapest.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rangers beaten by Robbie Keane's Ferencvaros

name: Red Star Belgrade at SK Sturm Graz

headlines.description: Wilfried Nancy became the first Celtic manager to lose his opening two games as Roma won 3-0 at Parkhead.
headlines.shortLinkText: Roma rout Celtic; Nancy makes unwanted history

headlines.description: Second-half substitute Youri Tielemans hit the winner to help Aston Villa move a step closer to automatic qualification in the Europa League with a 2-1 win at FC Basel.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa beat Basel, extend winning streak to 8 games

headlines.description: Ten-man Celtic lost a two-goal half-time lead but held on for what could be a precious Europa League point in Bologna.
headlines.shortLinkText: Celtic battle for Europa League draw at Bologna

headlines.description: Aston Villa guaranteed themselves a top-eight finish in the Europa League after Jadon Sancho's first-half header secured a 1-0 victory over Fenerbahce in Turkey.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sancho strike clinches Villa's UEL last-16 spot

name: SK Sturm Graz at Feyenoord Rotterdam
Red Star Belgrade at Malmö FF

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest's hopes of earning automatic qualification to the knockout rounds of the Europa League are all-but over after a disastrous 1-0 defeat at Braga.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest dealt Europa League blow with loss at Braga

headlines.description: Mohamed Diomande's first-half strike gave Rangers their first Europa League win of the season as they beat Ludogorets 1-0 at Ibrox.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rangers beat Ludogorets for 1st Europa League win

headlines.description: Nineteen-year-old substitute Jamaldeen Jimoh scored a late winner as Aston Villa came from two goals down to beat FC Salzburg 3-2 and finish in the Europa League top two amid further injury problems for boss Unai Emery.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jimoh strike seals Villa rally, second in UEL table

headlines.description: Celtic set up a Europa League play-off clash with either Ferencvaros or Stuttgart after beating Dutch side FC Utrecht 4-2 in their final group stage match at Celtic Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Celtic secure Europa League playoff spot with resounding win over Utrecht

headlines.description: Rangers first-half collapse saw them conclude their dismal Europa League campaign with a 3-1 defeat by Porto in the Estadio do Dragao.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rangers woeful UEL run ends with loss to Porto

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest will play Panathinaikos or Fenerbahce in the Europa League play-off round after ending their league phase with a 4-0 win over Robbie Keane's Ferencvaros.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest romp Ferencvaros to set up Europa League playoff tie

name: Celta Vigo at Red Star Belgrade
SK Brann at SK Sturm Graz

headlines.description: Vitor Pereira enjoyed a dream start at Nottingham Forest as his new side put one foot in the Europa League last 16 with a 3-0 win at Fenerbahce.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest rout Fenerbahce in dream start for Pereira

headlines.description: Kasper Schmeichel's every touch was booed by a section of Celtic fans in the aftermath of conceding two soft goals in a 4-1 Europa League defeat by Stuttgart.
headlines.shortLinkText: Stuttgart trounce Celtic to start Europa League playoff

notes.text: 2nd Leg - VfB Stuttgart advance 4-2 on aggregate
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - VfB Stuttgart advance 4-2 on aggregate

headlines.description: A much-changed Celtic team restored some pride with a 1-0 victory in Stuttgart as they exited the Europa League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Celtic fail to overcome Stuttgart deficit, exit UEL

notes.text: 2nd Leg - Tied on aggregate - Panathinaikos advance 4-3 on penalties
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - Tied on aggregate - Panathinaikos advance 4-3 on penalties

notes.text: 2nd Leg - Celta Vigo advance 3-1 on aggregate
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - Celta Vigo advance 3-1 on aggregate

notes.text: 2nd Leg - Nottingham Forest advance 4-2 on aggregate
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - Nottingham Forest advance 4-2 on aggregate

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest survived a scare against Fenerbahce in the Europa League playoff second leg as they booked their place in the last 16 with a 4-2 aggregate win.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest survive Fenerbahce scare to progress in UEL

notes.text: 2nd Leg - Racing Genk advance 6-4 on aggregate
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - Racing Genk advance 6-4 on aggregate

headlines.description: Ollie Watkins ended his goal drought as Aston Villa battled to a 1-0 away win at Lille in the first leg of their Europa League last 16 tie.
headlines.shortLinkText: Watkins nods Villa to UEL 1st-leg lead over Lille

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest suffered another Europa League defeat to Midtjylland after Cho Gue-sung's late header earned the Danish side a 1-0 win at the City Ground in the last-16 first leg.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Midtjylland winner sinks Forest first leg

notes.text: 2nd Leg - Tied on aggregate - Nottingham Forest advance 3-0 on penalties
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - Tied on aggregate - Nottingham Forest advance 3-0 on penalties

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest beat FC Midtjylland 3-0 on penalties to reach the Europa League quarterfinals on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest reach Europa quarters after penalties

notes.text: 2nd Leg - Celta Vigo advance 3-1 on aggregate
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - Celta Vigo advance 3-1 on aggregate

notes.text: 2nd Leg - SC Freiburg advance 5-2 on aggregate
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - SC Freiburg advance 5-2 on aggregate

notes.text: 2nd Leg - Aston Villa advance 3-0 on aggregate
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - Aston Villa advance 3-0 on aggregate

headlines.description: A moment of brilliance from Emiliano Martínez helped Aston Villa reach a third successive European quarterfinal as they saw off Lille in the Europa League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa ease past Lille to Europa quarterfinals

notes.text: 2nd Leg - FC Porto advance 4-1 on aggregate
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - FC Porto advance 4-1 on aggregate

notes.text: 2nd Leg - Real Betis advance 4-1 on aggregate
notes.headline: 2nd Leg - Real Betis advance 4-1 on aggregate

headlines.description: Ollie Watkins' second-half brace helped give Aston Villa a comfortable 3-1 lead over Bologna heading into next week's Europa League quarterfinal second leg at Villa Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Watkins brace puts Villa on brink of Europa semis

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest's Europa League hopes remain intact after a comical own goal helped them get out of their quarterfinal first leg at FC Porto with a 1-1 draw.
headlines.shortLinkText: Porto howler gifts Forest hope after UEL first leg
